page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-192GB is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor. It features 64 physical cores and 64 vCPUs with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing 3.0 GB of memory per core. It operates on a KVM hypervisor and utilizes a shared CPU allocation model. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance type, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance falls into the bottom 10%, and Geekbench text processing is in the bottom 25%. However, multi-core and parallelized execution show stronger results. The Passmark CPU score, extended instructions test, encryption test, and physics test all rank in the top 25%. Additionally, bzip3 multi-threaded decompression and Geekbench ray tracing benchmarks demonstrate strong performance in the top 25%. Most other metrics, including memory bandwidth, Redis SET operations, static web serving throughput, and software compilation, fall within the middle 50% average range.

The shared CPU allocation presents a clear tradeoff, as resource contention may affect workloads requiring sustained, predictable single-core performance. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high core count and memory capacity offer a dense resource profile suitable for parallelized tasks that can tolerate shared CPU limits. This server is suited for multi-threaded processing, batch compilation, ray tracing, and general-purpose workloads that leverage high core counts without requiring dedicated CPU pinning or local storage.
description: The UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-192GB is an x86_64 server featuring 64 physical AMD EPYC 7542 cores running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of RAM. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, this instance utilizes a shared CPU allocation model and does not include local storage or GPUs. Performance benchmarks show a significant variance: single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance, rank in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core and specialized tests, including Passmark CPU, encryption, and physics tests, place in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput remain in the average middle 50% range. Given the shared CPU architecture and high core count, this server is suited for highly parallelized workloads, multi-threaded batch processing, and general-purpose applications that do not require dedicated CPU resources or local storage.
